title: refactor: split enter frontend build
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Moves the enter dashboard into `frontend/` with a standalone frontend dev/build path while keeping the current Worker asset deployment.
- Narrows the dashboard-facing API surface through `frontend-api.ts` / `backend-types.ts` and typed Hono client calls.
- Moves reusable background job logic into `src/services/` so routes, scheduled handlers, and frontend API types stay less entangled.
- Keeps generated frontend Worker bindings as a temporary bridge until a generated API-client declaration package replaces live backend type imports.
- Fixes split dev: frontend Vite proxies same-origin `/api` to the Worker, while `config.apiBaseUrl` can use `VITE_API_BASE_URL` for future static/AWS hosting.
- Makes deploys deterministic by deploying the generated `dist/pollinations_enter/wrangler.json` config directly.

Addresses #11107

Checks:
- `npx tsc -p enter.pollinations.ai/frontend/src/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `npx tsc -p enter.pollinations.ai/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `npm run build:frontend`
- `npm run build:production`
- `npm run deploy:staging -- --dry-run`
- `npm run deploy:production -- --dry-run`
- `git diff --check`

gist.headline: Splitting the hive
gist.blurb: We've reorganized the enter dashboard's internal plumbing to give the frontend its own dedicated build path. Cleaner code means faster updates for the hive!
gist.summary: Moved the enter dashboard into a standalone frontend directory with its own Vite build path and narrowed the API surface to decouple it from the Worker backend.
gist.impact: Contributors will experience smoother local development via a dedicated Vite proxy and more deterministic deployments for the dashboard.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee organizing digital boxes or building a separate little room/shed for the frontend, representing the architectural split between frontend and backend.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny yellow hard hat, organizing glowing digital boxes into two separate neat piles. One pile has a retro computer monitor showing a colorful dashboard, the other shows glowing green backend server racks. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
